proven.claim: Prove: match[1] is never undefined when codeBlockRegex.exec() returns non-null.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3
; LINE: 14
; Prove: match[1] is never undefined when codeBlockRegex.exec() returns non-null.
; The regex has exactly one capture group ([\s\S]*?) which lazily matches
; zero or more chars — it is always present in the match array.
; Code transitions:
;   exec() returns non-null  →  exec_returned_null = 0
;   non-null match with one capture group  →  match1_defined = 1
(declare-const exec_returned_null Int)
(declare-const match1_defined Int)
; Transition: exec returning non-null guarantees capture group 1 exists
(assert (=> (= exec_returned_null 0) (= match1_defined 1)))
; Inside the while loop the guard guarantees exec returned non-null
(assert (= exec_returned_null 0))
; Negation of property: match[1] is undefined inside the loop
(assert (not (= match1_defined 1)))
(check-sat)
; expect unsat → match[1]! non-null assertion is always safe
